definition: Compare graph-level samples using Weisfeiler-Lehman labels, graph kernels, or Wasserstein distances between node-embedding distributions. This is distinct from node-neighborhood overlap: it audits generalization from whole training graphs to structurally novel evaluation graphs.
example_usage: python metric_to_similarity.py --input graph_kernel.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --metric-kind similarity --metric-col kernel

evidence.summary: The paper defines a graph Wasserstein distance over node feature distributions and improves graph classification.
It reviews WL subtree kernels and describes comparing larger graph substructures.
The graph Wasserstein distance is converted into a graph kernel similarity for learning.

limitations: Graph kernels can be expensive and sensitive to node/edge attributes and graph preprocessing.
Kernel similarity may not align with the scientific shift factor if labels or metadata define novelty.
The bundled example expects a precomputed kernel or distance matrix.
quality_gates: Report graph representation, node and edge attributes, WL iterations, distance metric, and kernel normalization.
Check whether graph size alone explains the similarity axis.
Compare graph-level kernel similarity to explicit domain or scaffold-like graph metadata when available.
